title: Email Validation Function
summary: A Python function that validates email addresses based on standard format rules and common best practices.

user_stories.title: Validate a valid email address
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to check if an email address is valid so that I can ensure it follows the correct format.
acceptance_criteria: The function returns True for a properly formatted email like 'test@example.com'
The function correctly identifies the local part, domain, and TLD

user_stories.title: Reject an invalid email address
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to detect invalid email addresses so that I can prevent incorrect data entry.
acceptance_criteria: The function returns False for an email like 'invalid-email@com'
The function returns False for an email with multiple '@' symbols

user_stories.title: Handle edge cases
user_stories.description: As a user, I want the function to handle edge cases such as leading or trailing spaces in the email.
acceptance_criteria: The function trims whitespace from the input before validation
The function returns False for emails with invalid characters

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The function should validate an email in under 1 millisecond
The function should not raise exceptions for any valid or invalid input
The function should be well-documented and easy to modify for future validation rules

constraints: The function must be implemented in Python
No external libraries are allowed for validation
assumptions: Email validation will follow standard RFC 5322 format rules
The function will not perform DNS or SMTP checks
out_of_scope: Checking if the email domain actually exists
Verifying if the email is deliverable
